commands.repair_reason: Maintainer acceptance of the scoped SSRF boundary relaxation remains the action; CI and contributor proof do not point to an automated repair.; Cleared: Security-sensitive diff reviewed; no new dependencies, scripts, credentials, or broad private-network access were added, and the SSRF relaxation is hostname-scoped. (sha=29d82dc31990534f0fb6880ab8dfcdc854b11dd5)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an api.openai.com-scoped fake-IP SSRF policy to OpenAI Realtime secret fetches, covers voice/transcription tests, and adds one Codex test type annotation.

PR surface: Source +7, Tests +15. Total +22 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source rather than a local live current-main run: current main sends Realtime secret requests through fetchWithSsrFGuard without the fake-IP policy, and the guard blocks RFC 2544/ULA resolved addresses unless policy opts in. The contributor also supplied after-fix Clash fake-IP proof.
commands.review_followup: Maintainer acceptance of the scoped SSRF boundary relaxation remains the action; CI and contributor proof do not point to an automated repair.

target.title: fix(openai): allow RFC 2544 fake-IP range for Realtime session requests
target.body: ## Summary - \`fetchWithSsrFGuard\` in \`createOpenAIRealtimeSecret\` was missing \`policy: { allowRfc2544BenchmarkRange: true }\`, causing the SSRF guard to block requests when proxy stacks (Clash, Surge, sing-box in TUN/fake-IP mode) resolve \`api.openai.com\` to the \`198.18.0.0/15\` special-use range. - The same opt-in already exists on other provider HTTP call paths; this fix closes the gap for the Realtime session-secret fetch path. - Added \`policy\` field assertions to both existing fetch-request checks in \`realtime-voice-provider.test.ts\` to prevent silent regression. Closes part of #56535. ## Verification \`\`\` pnpm test extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts \`\`\` - Test Files: 1 passed - Tests: 48 passed ## Real behavior proof Behavior addressed: SSRF guard blocks OpenAI Realtime session-secret requests when a TUN/fake-IP proxy resolves \`api.openai.com\` → \`198.18.0.0/15\` Real environment tested: macOS 15, OpenClaw 2026.5.25 (e3b9c11), Clash TUN/fake-IP mode active Exact steps or command run after this patch: \`OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw-dev pnpm gateway:dev --port 19001\`; open UI at \`http://127.0.0.1:19001\`; click Realtime Voice button Evidence after fix: \`talk.client.create\` RPC returned \`ok: true\` with \`durationMs: 1711\` (real network round-trip); audio streaming active with continuous \`talk.session.appendAudio\` calls all \`ok: true\`; AI responded in realtime — browser DevTools console: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/87a884d2-2902-4915-ade1-99993691d5da Observed result after fix: Realtime voice session fully established; no SSRF block error; AI conversation active (see screenshots below) What was not tested: live Surge / sing-box environments (same code path, same policy opt-in) **Browser DevTools — session creation (`talk.client.create` → `ok: true`):** 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: shushushv
